pr.title: items: match 19 functions across item files
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: PR #2258 decompiled 19 item functions across item files, updated several item headers and shared item variable structs, and was reported by the PR body and bot report as producing 19 new 100% matches.

key_files.role: shared item variable layout updates: added itLipstickSpore_ItemVars, exposed Tincle x4C, added pokemon xE44 int/float union, and typed Oldottosea x24
item type header touched; diff excerpt is truncated before exact added line
matched it_802C8C34 and it_2725_Logic80_PickedUp; added item/inlines includes
matched it_802AFEA8 dispatcher and updated helper prototypes in header
matched it_802C573C; added efLib_DestroyAll call and eflib include
matched itFlipper_UnkMotion5_Anim and itFlipper_UnkMotion6_Anim; header prototype for it_80291344 changed to bool Item_GObj*
matched it_802E31F8 item state transition and cleanup path
matched it_2725_Logic39_Destroyed using Pikachu SpecialLw helper calls
matched it_2725_Logic90_Destroyed with Popo/Kirby callback branch and owner clear
matched itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim; uses PAD_STACK(8) in matched body
matched it_802D2A58, setting effect hitlag callbacks and accessory callback
matched it_802B3F20 dynamic-bone position fetch; uses PAD_STACK(8)
matched it_802BFE5C, copying position into ItemLink velocity and applying facing direction to x velocity
matched it_802CB844 using switch on item kind to select velocity scale
matched it_802CA8DC; header prototypes updated; body still uses M2C_FIELD at offset 0xE3C
matched it_802E2E30 using newly typed oldottosea x24 field; header prototype updated
matched itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys using newly exposed Tincle x4C field; header prototype updated
matched it_802CA014 using pokemon xE44_s32 timer union arm; header prototypes updated
matched itLipstickspore_UnkMotion0_Phys using new lipstickspore item vars and explicit absolute-value pattern

what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments with C bodies for 19 item functions listed in the PR body.
Updated prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures in itflipper.h, itkabigon.h, itkamex.h, itseakneedlethrown.h, itoldottosea.h, and ittincle.h.
Added item variable layout information in itCommonItems.h for lipstick spore, Tincle, Pokemon xE44 int/float access, and Oldottosea x24.
Added cross-system includes required by matched item functions, including fighter character headers, ef/eflib.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, and item/inlines or item headers.
Bot report recorded 19 new matches with total matched code +1740 bytes and overall matched code 55.82% (+0.04%).

smart_moves.text: Recovered small item callbacks by connecting them to existing item state helpers such as Item_80268E5C, Item_8026A8EC, Item_802694CC, and effect helper callbacks instead of leaving isolated placeholder functions.
Paired body recovery with header prototype cleanup where helper signatures became clear.
Used typed item variable fields or union arms where the matched bodies showed field roles, including lipstickspore.xDD8, oldottosea.x24, tincle.x4C, and pokemon.xE44_s32.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item callback matches, updating the owning item-vars struct can unlock cleaner C bodies by replacing padding or ambiguous float-only fields with the access shape the function needs.
When an item function body proves helper signatures, clean the corresponding header prototypes in the same PR so callers no longer rely on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Small item logic functions often match as direct state transitions plus field cleanup: null checks, owner or effect cleanup, animation-state changes, and item-var flag resets.

naming_conventions.name: itLipstickSpore_ItemVars
naming_conventions.lesson: New item-var structs may use item-specific CamelCase type names while fields remain offset-style xDD4/xDD8 when semantics are not yet proven.

naming_conventions.name: xE44_s32
naming_conventions.lesson: When one item-var slot is accessed with multiple scalar interpretations, this PR used a union arm with a type suffix rather than renaming the field semantically.

naming_conventions.name: ip
naming_conventions.lesson: Recovered item functions consistently use ip for Item* locals obtained from GET_ITEM or gobj->user_data.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: PR evidence supports that these functions reached 100% according to the bot report, but no narrow objdiff rationale for the stack padding is included.
Matches loaded bodies to struct layout edits rather than leaving padding-only access.
Uses a compact switch on ItemKind 0xC1/0xC2 to select attribute scale before multiplying all velocity components.
Bodies use explicit if-negative-then-negate patterns rather than an ABS macro; PR evidence does not explain whether macro alternatives were tested.
The matched body still uses M2C_FIELD for an item offset; this is evidence of a bridge tactic, not evidence that the field is fully recovered.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported 19 new matches, all listed at 100.00%, with matched code 55.82% (+0.04%, +1740 bytes).
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.

follow_up_queries: Search current source for M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C) and nearby Kamex item-var layout to see whether a real field can replace the bridge access.
Search item files for PAD_STACK(8) in short animation/position helper functions to decide whether these two uses have been superseded or need documentation.
Check current itclinkmilk.c include block for duplicate it/inlines.h and it/item.h includes, because the PR diff shows them added twice in the excerpt.

Search itCommonItems.h current definitions for itLipstickSpore_ItemVars, itPokemon_ItemVars.xE44_s32, itTincle_ItemVars.x4C, and itOldottosea_ItemVars.x24 to see if later PRs renamed these fields semantically.
Search headers for remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in item helper prototypes adjacent to the functions touched by PR2258.

notes: The loaded PR body explicitly lists all 19 matched functions and claims ninja plus fuzzy_match_percent 100.0.
The bot issue comment independently reports 19 new 100.00% matches and matched-code byte gains.
The diff excerpt is truncated near src/melee/it/types.h, so the exact types.h addition is not visible in loaded evidence.
No human review comments are loaded; reviewer intent and any requested changes should not be inferred.
PAD_STACK and M2C_FIELD uses are visible in diff evidence, but no PR discussion explains why those tactics were required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Item matches can require item-vars layout recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2258 shows several item callback matches landing together with item-vars layout edits in itCommonItems.h, including new fields or union arms that are then used directly by matched item bodies.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Clean item helper prototypes when bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR updated item headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete Item_GObj/Fighter_GObj signatures alongside matched function bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: Document PAD_STACK evidence requirement with PR2258 examples
source_update_candidates.text: PR2258 includes PAD_STACK(8) in two matched item functions and bot evidence that the functions reached 100%, but no human rationale. This could be used as an example under the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard: keep stack padding narrow and attach bot/objdiff evidence plus cleanup follow-up.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides concrete examples of stack padding in matched item code, but standards should preserve the caveat that no explicit rationale was included.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR2258 as an item-vars typing example
source_update_candidates.text: PR2258 demonstrates replacing padding or ambiguous scalar access with item-var fields and union arms when a matched item function body proves the access pattern.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math guidance and gives item-specific examples.

source_update_candidates.title: Item callback and item-var layout files touched by PR2258
source_update_candidates.text: PR2258 matched item callbacks across src/melee/it/items and adjusted shared item variable structs in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for source-path indexing; should remain a path fact candidate rather than a decomp lesson.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that the remaining M2C_FIELD in itkamex is accepted long-term style; loaded evidence only shows it was present in a merged matched body.
Do not claim ABS macro alternatives were rejected for lipstickspore or nessyoyo; no PR evidence discusses macro testing.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or review feedback beyond the bot report because the loaded slice has zero review comments.

Do not treat the PR body's ninja/fuzzy-match claims as a complete verification ledger for every tactic; the bot report supports 19 100% matches but does not document local objdiff reasoning for PAD_STACK or M2C_FIELD.
